tasks.title: Initialize Vite + React 19 + TypeScript 5.6 project
tasks.description: Set up the frontend project skeleton using Vite 6, React 19, and TypeScript 5.6 as specified in the technical stack
tasks.details: npx create-vite@6 app --template react-ts; npm i react@19 react-dom@19 @types/react@19 @types/react-dom@19; npm i -D typescript@5.6 @types/node@22; Configure tsconfig.json with strict mode, moduleResolution: 'bundler', jsx: 'react-jsx'; Add vite.config.ts with React plugin and base '/'; Test with npm run dev
tasks.testStrategy: Verify app loads at localhost:5173 without errors; TypeScript compiles cleanly; Hot reload works on component changes

subtasks.title: Create Vite project with React-TS template
subtasks.description: Initialize the base Vite project using the specified command with Vite 6 and React-TypeScript template
subtasks.details: Run `npx create-vite@6 app --template react-ts` in the terminal. Navigate to the project directory with `cd app` and execute `npm install` to set up initial dependencies.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify project folder is created with src/, public/, vite.config.ts, tsconfig.json, and package.json containing Vite 6 and React dependencies

subtasks.title: Upgrade React to version 19 and TypeScript to 5.6
subtasks.description: Install React 19, related types, TypeScript 5.6, and Node types as development dependencies
subtasks.details: Execute `npm i react@19 react-dom@19 @types/react@19 @types/react-dom@19` followed by `npm i -D typescript@5.6 @types/node@22`. Check package.json to confirm versions are correctly installed.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `npm run build` to ensure TypeScript compiles without version conflicts or peer dependency warnings

subtasks.title: Configure tsconfig.json for strict mode and modern JSX
subtasks.description: Update tsconfig.json with strict mode, bundler module resolution, and React-jsx transform settings
subtasks.details: Edit tsconfig.json to include: `strict: true`, `moduleResolution: 'bundler'`, `jsx: 'react-jsx'`. Ensure `compilerOptions` includes `target: 'ES2022'`, `lib: ['ES2022', 'DOM', 'DOM.Iterable']`, and `skipLibCheck: true`.
subtasks.testStrategy: Create a sample .tsx file with modern JSX syntax and verify `tsc --noEmit` passes without errors

subtasks.title: Add and configure vite.config.ts with React plugin
subtasks.description: Create vite.config.ts file with React plugin and base path configuration
subtasks.details: Create `vite.config.ts` in root with: `import { defineConfig } from 'vite'; import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react'; export default defineConfig({ plugins: [react()], base: '/' });`. Ensure @vitejs/plugin-react is available.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `npm run dev` and verify Vite dev server starts at localhost:5173 with HMR enabled

subtasks.title: Test project setup with development server
subtasks.description: Verify complete project setup by running dev server and testing core functionality
subtasks.details: Execute `npm run dev` and open localhost:5173. Modify App.tsx (change title text), save, and confirm hot reload works. Check browser console for TypeScript or React errors.
subtasks.testStrategy: App loads without console errors; Hot Module Replacement updates changes instantly; TypeScript shows no compilation errors in IDE

tasks.title: Install Tailwind CSS v4 (Oxide engine) + shadcn/ui
tasks.description: Configure Tailwind CSS v4 and shadcn/ui with Radix primitives for component library
tasks.details: npx tailwindcss@4 init -p; Configure tailwind.config.js with OKLCH colors, content: ['./index.html', './src/**/*.{ts,tsx}'], theme.extend.colors; npx shadcn-ui@latest init; Add shadcn components: button, card, dialog; Set up globals.css with @tailwind directives and Inter font via @import url('https://rsms.me/inter/inter.css')
tasks.testStrategy: Verify Tailwind classes apply correctly; shadcn Button renders with correct styles; Dark mode toggle works via class strategy

subtasks.title: Install Tailwind CSS v4 with Oxide engine and initialize config
subtasks.description: Install Tailwind CSS v4 using the Oxide engine and generate the initial Tailwind configuration file.
subtasks.details: Run `npx tailwindcss@4 init -p` to create `tailwind.config.js` and set up the Oxide engine. Ensure the project is configured to use PostCSS and Tailwind as a PostCSS plugin.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify that `tailwind.config.js` is created and that Tailwind can be processed through PostCSS.

subtasks.title: Configure tailwind.config.js with OKLCH colors and content paths
subtasks.description: Update the Tailwind configuration to use OKLCH color space and define content paths for class detection.
subtasks.details: In `tailwind.config.js`, set `content: ['./index.html', './src/**/*.{ts,tsx}']` and configure `theme.extend.colors` using OKLCH color definitions. Ensure the configuration is valid and does not throw errors during build.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run a build or dev server and confirm no Tailwind config errors; verify that Tailwind classes are detected from the specified content paths.

subtasks.title: Initialize shadcn/ui and configure globals.css path
subtasks.description: Run the shadcn/ui init command and ensure it points to the correct globals.css file.
subtasks.details: Execute `npx shadcn-ui@latest init` and follow prompts, making sure the `globals.css` path is set to the actual file inside the `(app)` directory. Confirm that shadcn/ui generates or updates the necessary configuration files.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify that shadcn/ui configuration files are created or updated and that the `globals.css` path is correctly referenced.

subtasks.title: Add shadcn/ui components (button, card, dialog) and set up Inter font
subtasks.description: Install and configure the required shadcn/ui components and import the Inter font for consistent typography.
subtasks.details: Use the shadcn/ui CLI to add the `button`, `card`, and `dialog` components. In `globals.css`, add `@import url('https://rsms.me/inter/inter.css');` to load the Inter font and ensure the `@tailwind` directives (`@tailwind base; @tailwind components; @tailwind utilities;`) are present.
subtasks.testStrategy: Confirm that the components are imported and rendered correctly in a test component; verify that the Inter font is applied to the UI.

subtasks.title: Verify Tailwind and shadcn/ui integration with dark mode toggle
subtasks.description: Test that Tailwind classes and shadcn/ui components work correctly, including dark mode via class strategy.
subtasks.details: Create a simple test page that uses Tailwind utility classes and shadcn/ui components (e.g., Button). Implement a dark mode toggle that adds/removes a `dark` class on the root element and confirm that the theme changes as expected. Ensure that the `darkMode` setting in `tailwind.config.js` is configured to use the class strategy.
subtasks.testStrategy: Manually test the dark mode toggle and verify that Tailwind classes and shadcn/ui components render correctly in both light and dark modes; confirm that no styling issues occur.

tasks.title: [CANCELLED] Set up SQLocal + Drizzle ORM with OPFS persistence
tasks.description: [Superseded 2026-05-07 by tasks 54-58.] Original scope: local database layer using SQLite-WASM via SQLocal and Drizzle ORM with OPFS persistence. Cancelled because the architecture pivoted to a cloud DB (Cloudflare D1) — multi-device sync was promoted to a hard product requirement and OPFS-as-source-of-truth cannot meet it. The phase-0d-db-layer branch documents the implementation that was tried; tasks 54-58 cover the new path.
tasks.details: Cancelled. See PRD § 7.1 architecture pivot 2026-05-07. Successor tasks: 54 (Cloudflare + Wrangler), 55 (Clerk auth), 56 (Hono API), 57 (D1 + Drizzle migrations), 58 (typed API client).
tasks.testStrategy: n/a (cancelled)

subtasks.title: Install SQLocal, Drizzle ORM, and dev tools
subtasks.description: Install the required runtime and dev dependencies for SQLocal, Drizzle ORM, and migration tooling.
subtasks.details: Run `npm install sqlocal drizzle-orm` for runtime dependencies and `npm install -D drizzle-kit @libsql/client` for dev dependencies. Verify that all packages are listed in package.json.
subtasks.testStrategy: Check package.json for correct versions; ensure no install errors in CI or local dev.

subtasks.title: Initialize SQLocal with OPFS in db/index.ts
subtasks.description: Create the database entry point using SQLocal with OPFS persistence.
subtasks.details: Create `src/db/index.ts` and initialize SQLocal with `sqlocal('hp-coach.db', { useOPFS: true })`. Export a singleton database instance that will be used by Drizzle ORM.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify file is created; confirm no errors on first page load; check that OPFS is used via browser dev tools.

subtasks.title: Define initial Drizzle schema with users and sessions
subtasks.description: Create a Drizzle schema file defining the users and sessions tables.
subtasks.details: Create `src/db/schema.ts` using Drizzle ORM’s `sqliteTable`, `int`, `text`, etc. Define `users` and `sessions` tables with appropriate columns and constraints. Export the schema for use in migrations and queries.
subtasks.testStrategy: Ensure TypeScript compiles; confirm tables are defined with correct types.

subtasks.title: Configure and run Drizzle migrations with drizzle-kit
subtasks.description: Set up Drizzle Kit to generate and apply SQLite migrations for the local database.
subtasks.details: Configure `drizzle.config.ts` to point to the SQLocal database and schema. Run `drizzle-kit generate:sqlite` to create migration files, then apply them to the database. Ensure migrations create the users and sessions tables correctly.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify migrations run without errors; inspect database schema to confirm tables exist; ensure migrations are idempotent.

subtasks.title: Test OPFS persistence across page reloads
subtasks.description: Verify that data written to the SQLocal database persists across browser page reloads using OPFS.
subtasks.details: Write a small test script that inserts sample data into the users or sessions table, reloads the page, and queries the same data. Confirm that the data is still present and that queries execute within the expected performance threshold.
subtasks.testStrategy: Insert test data, reload page, verify data persists; ensure migration runs without errors; confirm queries execute under 50ms.

tasks.title: Configure PWA with vite-plugin-pwa
tasks.description: Make app installable PWA with offline support and service worker caching
tasks.details: npm i vite-plugin-pwa@0.20; Add to vite.config.ts: defineConfig({ plugins: [react(), VitePWA({ registerType: 'autoUpdate', workbox: { globPatterns: ['**/*.{js,css,html,ico,png,svg}'] }, manifest: { name: 'HP-Coach', short_name: 'HPCoach', icons: [...] } })] }); Test offline mode after first load
tasks.testStrategy: Install PWA prompt appears; App works offline after cache; Service worker updates on new deploy

subtasks.title: Install vite-plugin-pwa dependency
subtasks.description: Add the vite-plugin-pwa package to the project dependencies using the specified version for PWA functionality.
subtasks.details: Run `npm i vite-plugin-pwa@0.20` to install the plugin. Verify installation by checking package.json and node_modules.
subtasks.testStrategy: Check package.json includes vite-plugin-pwa@0.20; Run `npm ls vite-plugin-pwa` to confirm no errors.

subtasks.title: Create PWA icons and place in public folder
subtasks.description: Generate required app icons (192x192, 512x512, maskable) and place them in the public directory for the manifest.
subtasks.details: Create icons named pwa-192x192.png, pwa-512x512.png, maskable-icon-512x512.png in /public/. Use proper sizes and formats as per PWA standards.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify icons exist in /public/ with correct filenames and sizes; Icons should be visible in generated manifest.

subtasks.title: Configure VitePWA plugin in vite.config.ts
subtasks.description: Update vite.config.ts to include VitePWA plugin with manifest, workbox caching, and autoUpdate registration.
subtasks.details: Add import { VitePWA } from 'vite-plugin-pwa'; Update defineConfig plugins array with VitePWA({ registerType: 'autoUpdate', workbox: { globPatterns: ['**/*.{js,css,html,ico,png,svg}'] }, manifest: { name: 'HP-Coach', short_name: 'HPCoach', icons: [...] } }).
subtasks.testStrategy: Build project without config errors; Check generated /dist/sw.js and manifest.json contain correct configurations.

subtasks.title: Build and serve PWA for service worker registration
subtasks.description: Build the production version and serve over HTTPS to enable service worker registration and caching.
subtasks.details: Run `npm run build` then serve dist/ folder using `npx serve -s dist --ssl-cert cert.pem --ssl-key key.pem` or vite preview with HTTPS. First load should register service worker.
subtasks.testStrategy: Chrome DevTools > Application > Service Workers shows registered SW; Manifest parsed correctly with icons and app name.

subtasks.title: Test PWA installability and offline functionality
subtasks.description: Verify install prompt appears, app works offline after caching, and service worker updates on new builds.
subtasks.details: Load app online first time to cache assets. Test: 1) Install prompt appears; 2) Disconnect internet, reload - app loads from cache; 3) Deploy new build, verify autoUpdate refreshes SW.
subtasks.testStrategy: PWA install banner shows; Offline mode serves cached app; Console shows 'New content available' on updates; All routes/assets load offline.

tasks.title: Cloudflare account + Wrangler bootstrap

Create CF API token (Wrangler scope) and store in `.env.example` as documentation, plus GitHub Actions secret `CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN`. Login: `wrangler login` (or use API token in CI). Create empty Worker entry: `worker/src/index.ts` returning `new Response('hpc-api ok')`. Verify with `wrangler dev` locally and `wrangler deploy` to a staging route.

tasks.title: Clerk auth integration (sign-in / sign-up + route guards)
tasks.description: Drop in Clerk for production-grade auth — MFA, passkeys, WebAuthn, audit logs, brute-force lockout, leaked-password check — and gate the SPA routes behind it.
tasks.details: Create Clerk account; create application 'HP-Coach'. `pnpm --dir app add @clerk/clerk-react`. In `main.tsx` wrap RouterProvider with `<ClerkProvider publishableKey>`. Create `app/src/auth/` with: a `/sign-in` route, a `/sign-up` route (Clerk's prebuilt components), and a route guard hook that uses `<SignedIn>` / `<SignedOut>` to redirect to `/sign-in` for protected routes. Server side: install `@clerk/backend`, write a Hono middleware that verifies the bearer JWT on every API request and exposes `c.var.userId` (Clerk user id). Add `CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY` (frontend env), `CLERK_SECRET_KEY` (worker secret via `wrangler secret put`). Configure social login (Google) and magic-link email in the Clerk dashboard; require email verification.
tasks.testStrategy: Unauthenticated visit to `/` redirects to `/sign-in`; sign-in via email magic link succeeds; `/api/me` returns 401 without bearer token, 200 with valid Clerk JWT; signing in on a second device shares the same Clerk user id; MFA enrollment completes for an opted-in user

tasks.title: Hono API skeleton on Cloudflare Workers
tasks.description: Stand up the typed RPC layer that owns every read/write against the cloud DB. Hono on Workers, Clerk JWT-verified, with structured error handling and rate limiting.
tasks.details: `worker/src/index.ts` mounts a Hono app. Routes scaffolded under `worker/src/routes/`: `health`, `me/prefs`, `me/export`, `sessions`, `attempts`, `mistakes`, `framework_progress`. Middleware order: cors → rate-limit (Cloudflare KV-backed counter, 60 rps per Clerk user) → Clerk JWT verify → request logger → handler. Errors normalised to `{ error: { code, message } }` JSON via Hono's error handler. End-to-end types: install `hono` + `@hono/zod-validator`, validate all inputs; export an `AppType` for the SPA's typed client (task 58). Wrangler.toml binds D1, KV, R2 via interface declared in `worker/src/types.ts`.
tasks.testStrategy: GET /health returns 200; missing/invalid JWT returns 401; rate-limit returns 429 after threshold; Zod validation rejects malformed bodies; integration tests via Miniflare exercise each route under a fake authenticated user

tasks.title: Cloudflare D1 database + Drizzle migrations
tasks.description: Provision the production + staging D1 databases, port the Drizzle schema, and wire migrations through Wrangler.
tasks.details: `wrangler d1 create hpc-prod` and `hpc-staging`. Bind both to the worker (env=production / env=staging). Move `app/src/db/schema.ts` (drafted on the cancelled task 3 branch) to `worker/src/db/schema.ts`. Add a `user_id` foreign key to every per-user table (sessions, attempts, mistakes, srs_state, mastery, framework_progress) so server-side authorization can scope queries. `drizzle.config.ts`: dialect=sqlite, out=./worker/drizzle. `pnpm db:generate` emits 0000_initial.sql; `wrangler d1 migrations apply hpc-staging` and verify on staging before applying to prod. Drizzle handle in worker: `drizzle(c.env.DB, { schema })` from `drizzle-orm/d1`. Indexes: (user_id, layer1_id), (user_id, due_at), (user_id, started_at).
tasks.testStrategy: `wrangler d1 execute hpc-staging --command 'SELECT name FROM sqlite_master'` lists all 7 tables; cross-user select returns zero rows for the wrong user_id; EXPLAIN on the mistake-queue query uses the (user_id, layer1_id) index; migrations idempotent on re-apply

tasks.title: Typed API client + TanStack Query hooks
tasks.description: Generate a fully typed client from Hono's AppType and expose it as TanStack Query hooks the screens can call directly.
tasks.details: `pnpm --dir app add hono` (peer of @hono/client). Create `app/src/api/client.ts` that imports `AppType` from `worker/src/index.ts` (path import via tsconfig path) and instantiates `hc<AppType>(BASE_URL, { headers: () => ({ Authorization: `Bearer ${await clerk.session.getToken()}` }) })`. Hooks live in `app/src/api/hooks/`: useUserPrefs, useSetUserPrefs, useSessions, useStartSession, useEndSession, useRecordAttempt, useMistakes, useFrameworkProgress. Mutations invalidate the relevant query keys. Optimistic updates for prefs only; the rest goes server-first to avoid cross-device confusion. TanStack Query default: 5-minute stale time on read queries, refetchOnWindowFocus=true.
tasks.testStrategy: Component test renders <ProvenComponent /> wrapped in a QueryClient and asserts useUserPrefs returns the row. End-to-end Playwright: change a pref on device A simulator, navigate on device B simulator (different localStorage), see the new value after the refetch window

tasks.title: Refactor screens + Zustand stores to use the API client
tasks.description: Replace the placeholder local-store state in Zustand with API-backed reads/writes. The cloud DB becomes the source of truth; Zustand owns only ephemeral UI state.
tasks.details: Sweep app/src/stores/{coachStore,examStore,uiStore}.ts. coachStore.coach: read from useUserPrefs, write via useSetUserPrefs. examStore.sittingId: same pattern. uiStore (palette/mode/font/density): keep persisted to localStorage AND mirror to user prefs server-side (settings sync without blocking the toggle). HomeMobile.tsx already reads from these stores via hooks, so the screen file barely changes. Onboarding writes initial prefs through useSetUserPrefs. Stub routes (drill/coach/progress/avancerat) get sign-in protection via the Clerk route guard.
tasks.testStrategy: Vitest: store hooks return the API-backed values when wrapped in a QueryClientProvider with mocked fetch. Playwright: sign in as user A, set coach to 'professor', sign out, sign in as user B, see default 'taktiker'. Sign back in as A from a fresh browser context, see 'professor' restored

tasks.title: [deferred to Phase 1] Offline cache + mutation queue (Dexie)
tasks.description: Layer Dexie/IndexedDB on the SPA as a write-through cache so the app keeps working offline and queues mutations until reconnect.
tasks.details: PRD § 9.15 commits this to Phase 1, not v0. When activated: Dexie store mirroring the read query keys; TanStack Query `persister` plugin for cache rehydration; mutation queue table writes pending mutations on offline error and drains via a Service Worker background-sync handler on reconnect. Conflict policy: last-write-wins for single-row state (prefs, mastery), append-only for attempts/mistakes (no conflicts possible). Network-status indicator in the bottom tab bar.
tasks.testStrategy: Acceptance: a user on a flight can answer questions, the app reports 'offline (queued)', and on reconnect everything reconciles without prompting. E2E with Playwright `context.setOffline(true)` covering submit-then-reconnect.

tasks.title: [deferred to Phase 1] Live cross-device sync via Cloudflare Durable Objects
tasks.description: Push channel so a write on one signed-in device propagates to the user's other devices within a second, not 30 seconds.
tasks.details:
Today's cross-device story uses TanStack Query's refetchOnWindowFocus + a 30s refetchInterval on useActiveSession (added in task 59). That's 'calm sync' — fine for dogfood, perceptibly slow for real use. Phase 1 promotes this to push:

Architecture: one Durable Object per Clerk user (id = clerkUserId). The Worker subscribes each device's WebSocket / SSE connection on sign-in. Mutating endpoints (POST /api/sessions, PATCH /api/sessions/:id, PATCH /api/me/prefs, future POST /api/sessions/:id/attempts) broadcast a small {type, key} message via the DO; subscribers invalidate the corresponding TanStack Query keys.

Fallback: SSE first (one-way, simpler, works through proxies). WebSocket only if bidirectional commands turn up. Heartbeat every 25s; auto-reconnect with backoff.

UI: subtle live-status dot in the bottom-tab bar — green when connected, yellow when reconnecting. Don't block the UI on disconnect.
tasks.testStrategy: Two Playwright contexts signed in as the same Clerk user. Context A starts a session and increments position; context B observes the active-session widget within ≤ 2s without an explicit refetch trigger. Disconnect/reconnect resilience: drop the WebSocket on B, wait 5s, observe reconnection without losing state.
